suggestions.topic: Glamsterdam devnet-7 status update and stability assessment
suggestions.rationale: Devnet-7 was designated as the 'final devnet' with a mild spec freeze. Launched July 13-14, it needs a comprehensive stability check before proceeding to public testnet planning.

suggestions.topic: Public testnet timeline and readiness criteria
suggestions.rationale: With devnet-7 as the final devnet, teams need to establish clear criteria for declaring it ready and timeline for launching Glamsterdam on public testnet (Holesky, Sepolia).

suggestions.topic: EIP-8037 block-level floor cost fix verification
suggestions.rationale: Critical bug allowing 8x expected max block size was fixed in spec 7.2 and included in devnet-7. Need confirmation that all clients implemented correctly and no edge cases remain.

suggestions.topic: SSZ Engine API implementation progress and breakout call takeaways
suggestions.rationale: Breakout call scheduled for July 11 (Friday after ACDT #86). Teams should share implementation status, spec alignment progress, and any blockers identified during the breakout.

suggestions.topic: PTC security concern review from CL breakout
suggestions.rationale: Deferred from ACDT #84 on June 22 to CL breakout. Concern about voting on non-canonical forks was flagged as potential ePBS readiness blocker but hasn't been revisited on testing calls.
Raised by Besu in ACDT #84 as needed before declaring any 'final devnet'. With devnet-7 now in that role, explicit confirmation of testing scope completion is needed.

suggestions.topic: EIP cleanup proposals for devnet-8 (execution gas terminology, log cost consolidation)
suggestions.rationale: Approved in principle for devnet-8 in ACDT #87 but marked as spec-text-only changes. May not need extensive discussion unless implementation concerns arise.

suggestions.topic: EL test suite status across all clients
suggestions.rationale: ACDT #87 noted Besu failing ~40 repricing tests, Geth with mismatched error messages, and Reth branch delayed. Update on resolution status would confirm testing infrastructure is solid.
